title: acrosanthes teretifolia
intro: Acrosanthes teretifolia, also known as the prickly bush, is a type of flowering plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Malpighiaceae. The medicinal properties of Acrosanthes teretifolia are primarily attributed to its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Acrosanthes teretifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Acrosanthes teretifolia typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
